items.title: Kumanjayi Little Baby remembered across NT after 'unthinkable trauma'

children.content: The Northern Territory is continuing to grieve the short life of a five-year-old girl today, with crowds in Alice Springs to pay tribute by donning pink — her favourite colour.

gallery.altText: Flowers on a fence with a note reading fly high baby girl
Two people standing in the water, holding one another with their eyes closed.
An Indigenous person wearing traditional face paint and touching another person's face as they stand in water at the beach.

thumbnail.altText: Flowers on a fence with a note reading fly high baby girl
Two people standing in the water, holding one another with their eyes closed.
An Indigenous person wearing traditional face paint and touching another person's face as they stand in water at the beach.

items.title: As fuel costs soar, cycling advocates argue our priorities are all wrong

children.content: Bike shops in Darwin are experiencing soaring demand as more people turn to one of the oldest and simplest alternatives to cars amid skyrocketing fuel prices, but advocates say the federal government is still prioritising drivers.

image.altText: A man smiles in a bike workshop.

items.title: Police charge Jefferson Lewis, 47, with 5yo girl's murder

children.content: Northern Territory Police have charged Jefferson Lewis with the murder of five-year-old Kumanjayi Little Baby in Alice Springs.

image.altText: A mugshot of an Aboriginal man, serious expression looking directly at camera, yellow / orange pigment behind brown eyes.

items.title: Police release CCTV of 'outright looting' of Alice Springs businesses

children.content: NT Police have released CCTV footage of the "outright looting" of businesses caught up in rioting following the arrest of Jefferson Lewis for the murder of Kumanjayi Little Baby.

image.altText: A large group of people swarms into a service station

articles.title: After Little Baby's death, Canavan demands government stop 'running scared'

children.content: Nationals leader Matthew Canavan says after the death of Kumanjayi Little Baby, the government must "move on" from its defeat at the Voice referendum and re-engage on Indigenous policy debate.

image.altText: Nationals Leader Matt Canavan gesturing while wearing a suit, with a screen behind him.

articles.title: Kumanjayi Little Baby's grandfather thanks community, says time to 'let justice take its course'

children.content: The family of five-year-old Kumanjayi Little Baby, whose body was found on the outskirts of Alice Springs on Thursday, has thanked the community for their support as tributes flow in from across the country.

image.altText: A teddy bear among floral tributes

gallery.altText: A large group of people swarms into a service station

thumbnail.altText: A large group of people swarms into a service station

items.title: Darwin club breaks record at Lake Argyle after man bitten by crocodile

children.content: Four people were bitten by a suspected freshwater crocodile just a day before a sold-out swimming competition was run in the same far-north Western Australia lake.

image.altText: A freshwater crocodile resting next to a lake

items.title: What is a town camp and what is life like there for Aboriginal people?

children.content: Kumanjayi Little Baby is believed to have been taken from her bed at night while visiting a town camp near Alice Springs with her mother. This is how town camps came to be.

image.altText: A drone shot of saltbush and roads in Alice Springs

items.title: Seeing Kakadu through the lens of Crocodile Dundee

children.content: Crew members recount filming scenes in some of Kakadu National Park's most iconic locations, forty years on from Crocodile Dundee's release.

gallery.altText: Man holds crocodile around the neck and smile sitting on bank of waterway

items.title: Three die after suspected hantavirus outbreak on cruise ship

children.content: A suspected outbreak of hantavirus, which is spread by contact with rat and mice faeces, has killed three people on a cruise ship.

image.altText: A cruise ship on the open ocean with a smaller dinghy approaching.

items.title: Daughter hopes $1m reward will solve mystery of mother's disappearance

children.content: Marion Barter has not been seen since 1997. Her daughter is determined to find out what happened to her mother, who she believes may have found herself "in a situation she did not fully control".

image.altText: Woman with dark hair poses with two young children by her side

items.title: Traffic gridlock as park visitors turn out to see the turning of the fagus

children.content: Nature lovers who went to see the autumn colours of the fagus at Tasmania's Mt Field National Park on Saturday say there were no car spaces and nowhere to turn around.

image.altText: Yellow fagus leaves on a tree.

items.title: Calls for 'compassion' after newborn twin dies at homeless camp

children.content: A women's health advocate says the death of a baby during childbirth at a homeless camp on the Murrumbidgee River is a "terrible day" for the Wagga Wagga community.

image.altText: View of Wagga beach signage from a footbath, looking towards a playground.

items.title: Russian woman's husband beat her but police were no help, so she wrote to Putin

children.content: Domestic violence and abuse have been described as an "epidemic" in multiple countries, but analysts argue there is something that sets Russia apart: the government is making things worse.

items.title: Chook left in oven, lost remote among 'absurd' reasons for Triple Zero calls

items.title: It was a record race time. Moments later spectators were suspicious

children.content: Tasmania's top horse race was run 37 metres shorter than advertised. Officials knew the track distance was likely incorrect but proceeded anyway. This is what happened.

image.altText: Elevated view of horse racing event with crowd looking on.

items.title: Rice is cheap, easy to cook and the main ingredient in these four meals

children.content: Rice doesn't need to play second fiddle. The cheap and simple pantry staple can hold up a whole meal.

items.title: A hacker built a tool to track police officers. Then he tried to warn them

items.title: Island residents fight land valuations as authorities block building work
